course_description_summary: This course is intended to introduce students to the game of tennis.  The focus of the course is on the development of necessary knowledge and fundamental skills required to safely and effectively play tennis. Topics covered include understanding rules, scoring, and etiquette of tennis as well as instruction of basic strokes. This course is designed for individuals who have had little to no previous tennis experience. (See the NTRP: National Tennis Rating Program at this link www.usta.com/Adult-Tennis/USTA-League/ntrp/  It is the basis by which we can determine skill level even if there is no USTA league or tournament experience.)                                                        Beginning skills include levels 1.5 – 2.5. course_description_summary: Students will be introduced to walking as a cardiovascular exercise related to a healthy lifestyle and improved quality of life.  Several campus walks designed to improve physical, mental, and emotional well-being will be introduced.  The student will learn proper walking posture and gait as well as appropriate warm up and cool down activities.  A variety of health and wellness issues will be addressed including the components of physical fitness, nutrition for exercise, as well as strength, flexibility, and recovery modalities to improve the walking experience.  Students will be expected to keep track of their walking through the blog feature in Canvas. Student Learning Outcomes: •	Gain an understanding of the importance of lifetime fitness. •	Acquire proper biomechanics of walking. •	Engage in proper warm up and cool down activities....

course_description_summary: This course is intended to introduce students to the activity of social dancing.  The focus of the course will be to develop the necessary skills to perform traditional ballroom dances such as the Fox Trot, Waltz, Rumba, Tango, Swing, and Cha-Cha along with an introduction to dance forms from a variety of cultures.  Through engaging in social dance, students will learn how to be a successful follower as well as a successful leader, increase confidence levels, and build upon social skills.  Students will also practice patience and perseverance throughout the semester as well as how to adjust and adapt quickly to different situations. Most importantly, students will take with them the knowledge and skill of lifetime fitness through social dance. Student Learning Outcomes •	Execute the proper dance positions for dancing with a partner. •	Demonstrate the basics steps in all dances. •	Identify the correct music and timing for each dance....

assignments_summary: What I Expect from Students; It is easy to get distracted in lecture. I ask you to try your best to remain focused and engaged during class. I expect you to take an active role in learning by coming to class meetings prepared and ready to collaborate with your classmates. Keep in mind that each member of this class has different ideas and perspectives that will enrich the experience for us all. I expect all of us to speak and listen with compassion and not make assumptions about others. This class should challenge you, but I KNOW everyone can succeed.; Respect for Diversity Statement; It is my intent that students from all diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and situations be well served by this course, that students’ learning needs be addressed both in and out of class, and that the diversity that students bring to this class be viewed as a resource, strength, and benefit. It is my intent to present materials and activities that are respectful of diversity, which may include (but not limited to): gender, sexuality, disability, age, socioeconomic status, ethnicity, race, religion, political affiliation, and culture. I acknowledge that there is likely to be a diversity of access to resources among students and plan to support all of you as best as I can. Please let me know ways to improve the effectiveness of the course for you and for other students (or student groups). In addition, if any of our class meetings conflict with your religious events, please let me know so that we can make appropriate arrangements. All people have the right to be addressed and referred to in accordance with their personal identity. In this class, we will have the chance to indicate the name that we prefer to be called and, if we choose, to identify pronouns with which we would like to be addressed. I will do my best to address and refer to all students accordingly and support classmates in doing so as well.; Course Calendar			 Z/C = Zumdahl & DeCoste Textbook.; Week; Monday; Wednesday; Associated Reading; 1

course_description_summary: At 700B  USD in annual revenue, the US Sports Industry represents 4% of the country’s Gross Domestic Product. It is 4X the airline industry, 6X the auto industry and 7X the movie industry. With the average salary of the NFL/NBA/MLB players at $4MM and coaches for the three sports averaging $3MM this is the highest paying profession in the world. This is no longer a pastime for a dedicated fan base but a powerful industry affecting all levels of participation. From NBA and NFL lockouts, to paying college athletes, to the forced sale of the LA Dodgers by MLB due to a divorce decree,  to Congressional hearings on steroids and a BCS playoff system, the Sports Industry represents a unique industry with unique constraints. Players, owners, managers, front office, agents, fans, media, sponsors, advertisers, it is a uniquely dynamic and interrelated industry that is growing at an exponential rate to the rest of the economy. We have multiple objectives for this class: To provide a broad overview of the industry/business that is Sports. Finance and valuation, professional and college, sponsorship and marketing, teams and leagues, labor and ownership, leagues and events, we will examine multiple aspects that define the landscape that is sports. We will discuss current events and topics each week applying what we learn. To examine the industry with a true finance perspective in determining valuations. We all love sports but how viable are these businesses and would we invest? The Green Bay Packers sold $50MM worth of stock in two days, good investment? To discuss the major topics that surround the industry: lockouts, free agency, paying college athletes, NCAA Tournament, College Athletic Budgets, Super Bowl, Professional Athlete salaries, Occupy NBA/Occupy NFL/Occupy MLB, Fantasy Football, Public Funding/Corporate Funding of Stadiums/Arenas, etc...

course_description_summary: Learning occurs best when there is distributed practice with immediate feedback in a welcoming and inclusive space. In large lecture courses, it can be difficult for one professor to provide individual attention to each student. Teaching interns can aid learning for all students in several ways. They are "near peers" and thus they can often better connect to a learner's confusion and intimidation with new material than the expert teaching the course. They can also move around the classroom during activities and talk with individuals and groups of students to provide immediate feedback. Lastly, providing advice about success, the teaching interns help provide a welcoming and inclusive environment for learning. Teaching interns will be part of a collaborative instructional team, under the supervision of a faculty member. Often, the intern would have taken the course whose instructional team they have joined in a previous semester and be familiar with the student experience and the course content. The teaching intern assists undergraduate students with learning inside and/or outside the classroom, depending on the needs of tat specific course. Responsibilities will vary depending on the course but may include performing demonstrations, facilitating group work, building curricular materials, and providing feedback to the students and instructional team. Professionalism is expected and interns will meet routinely with their faculty internship supervisor to receive advice and feedback about their performance. Because this is experiential learning for the teaching intern, routine reflection is expected. Course Goals and Objectives: As an internship experience, you are expected to attend the class you are an intern for, show up on time, be prepared for the content and activities for the day, abide by student privacy rules (FERPA), and be a professional, trustworthy member of the teaching team. Additionally the 7 goals are to:...

assignments_summary: Department consent required.; This course is a Duke service-learning course: The students engage in planned service activities for a minimum of 20 hours. The service experience is integrally related to the academic subject matter of the course. The coursework involves critical reflection on the relationship between academic course content and the service experience as well as the ethical and civic dimensions of the service experience.

course_description_summary: This course is a semester-long inquiry into a very specific kind of writing–research writing–that is designed to acclimate you to academic writing conventions more broadly and writing conventions in particular disciplines more specifically. There are three reasons I’ve designed this course. First, after taking required first-year writing courses, there are very few opportunities for students to work deliberately on their academic writing skills.
